fresh_facts.claim: US Central Command implemented a full naval blockade of Iranian ports and key shipping routes in the Strait of Hormuz, with at least 23 Iran-bound ships turned back since earlier in the week.
The US plans to board and seize Iran-linked commercial vessels in international waters beyond the Middle East to increase economic pressure.
Approximately 10,000 additional US troops are deploying to the Middle East by the end of April.
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated on April 17 that the US can transition from blockade to major combat operations.
Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and warned of full shutdown if the US blockade persists.
Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned of Hormuz closure in response to the US blockade on April 18.
Two Indian-linked vessels reportedly came under fire near the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran rejected a second round of US talks in a 21-hour Islamabad session led by VP JD Vance, accusing the US of excessive demands on the nuclear program while maintaining military pressure.
US-Iran ceasefire talks in Islamabad collapsed on April 11 after internal Iranian disagreements and an order from top security officials.
President Trump stated talks with Iran could resume over the next two days following the failed Islamabad negotiations.
Iran's central bank warned President Pezeshkian that rebuilding the war-damaged economy may take over a decade.
Iran's economy shows structural symptoms of currency distrust and monetary regime shift beyond high inflation.
President Pezeshkian faces fierce backlash and complete political deadlock from hardliners and the IRGC over his conditional war-end offer.
IRGC commanders forced the appointment of Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as Pezeshkian's SNSC secretary.
Supreme Leader Khamenei appointed Major General Amir Hatami as Artesh commander, replacing Mousavi.
Hardliners branded former Foreign Minister Zarif a traitor warranting arrest alongside former President Rouhani.
Israel's June 14 strikes hit critical energy infrastructure including the Shahran oil depot, degrading military and nuclear sustainment capabilities.
Israeli strikes targeted regime institutions including the Justice Ministry, Foreign Ministry, and internal security forces (MOIS and LEC anti-riot units).
Israeli PM Netanyahu confirmed a June 15 strike killed IRGC Intelligence Chief Kazemi, deputy Mohaghegh, and officer Bagheri.
The IDF expanded operations eastward to Mashhad airbase for air superiority.
Iran's Axis proxies have not significantly intervened in response to Israeli military operations.
State TV warned against revealing officials' civilian hideouts and framed dissent as enemy warfare.
A pro-government rally screened a Lebanese singer's video that broke hijab and solo female singing taboos.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces imminent April 22 ceasefire deadline amid comprehensive US military escalation (10,000+ additional troops, 3 carrier strike groups, naval blockade of 23+ vessels) and devastating Israeli strikes on June 14-15 targeting energy infrastructure and IRGC leadership, while regime fractures between Pezeshkian's diplomatic overtures and hardline IRGC control forcing succession changes and sidelining reformists. Second-round ceasefire talks in Islamabad collapsed on April 19 with Iran rejecting US demands, while economic reconstruction faces decade-long timeline amid currency distrust and monetary regime shift beyond hyperinflation.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli strikes on June 15 killed IRGC Intelligence Chief Mohammad Kazemi, deputies, and officers while targeting energy infrastructure, judicial institutions, and internal security apparatus across eastern Iran including Mashhad airbase, alongside US Defense Secretary confirmation of transition capability from blockade to major combat operations.
